name: Río Ulla – Xirimbao trail
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza are defined by the Ulla and Deza rivers, which create extensive river networks and varied natural corridors. The region features well-preserved riparian forests with diverse tree species, alongside traditional Galician countryside. A notable natural attraction is the Toxa Waterfall, one of Galicia's highest free-falling waterfalls, plunging over 30 meters amidst native forest and granite rock. Best waterfall hiking trails in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza The most popular waterfall hiking route is Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop Trail, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Ulla River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Fervenza do Río Pereiro loop from Avenida do Mestre Manuel Gómez Lorenzo, a difficult 12.9 miles (20.7 km) path. This trail explores the area around the Fervenza do Río Pereiro waterfall. Local hikers also love the Fervenza do Río Pereiro loop from O Matelo, a 11.6 miles (18.7 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Hiking in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza is defined by riverine ecosystems, lush riparian forests, and the dramatic Toxa Waterfall. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 580 reviews. More than 3800 hikers have used komoot to explore Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza offers a selection of 12 dedicated waterfall hiking trails. These routes vary in length and difficulty, providing options for different preferences.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on waterfall hikes in this region?
A: The region is defined by the Ulla and Deza rivers, creating extensive river networks and lush riparian forests. You'll encounter diverse tree species like alder, willow, and ash, alongside oaks and hollies. The most prominent feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3855179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Toxa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, one of Galicia's highest free-falling waterfalls.

Q: Is the Fervenza do Toxa Waterfall accessible, and how high is it?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3855179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Toxa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a standout attraction and is considered one of Galicia's highest free-falling waterfalls, plunging over 30 meters. It's often connected by hiking trails to the nearby Monastery of Carboeiro, making it accessible for combined natural and historical exploration.

Q: Are there any family-friendly waterfall hikes in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy waterfall hiking trail available in the Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza region. These easier paths are generally suitable for families looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on waterfall trails in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: The Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza region, as a Special Zone of Conservation (ZEC), generally welcomes responsible dog owners. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions, especially around sensitive natural areas or protected wildlife habitats.

Q: What is the best time of year to go waterfall hiking in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: The best time to visit for waterfall hiking is typically during the wetter seasons, such as late autumn, winter, and spring, when the rivers are full and the waterfalls are at their most powerful. The lush riparian forests are also vibrant during these times. Summer offers pleasant hiking weather, but waterfalls might have reduced flow.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes that incorporate waterfalls. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14984854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Río Pereiro loop from Avenida do Mestre Manuel Gómez Lorenzo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20494234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Río Pereiro loop from O Matelo&lt;/a&gt; both explore the area around the Fervenza do Río Pereiro waterfall as part of a loop.

Q: What kind of historical or cultural attractions can I find near these waterfall trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore significant historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3855179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Toxa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is directly connected by trails to the historic Monastery of Carboeiro. You might also encounter traditional Galician manor houses (pazos) and ancient structures like the Roman-origin &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2794030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Padrón Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1859587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontevea Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of waterfall hikes in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: The waterfall hiking trails in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 1 easy route, 5 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes. This range ensures options for both casual walkers and experienced hikers seeking a challenge.

Q: What do other hikers say about the waterfall trails in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: The komoot community highly rates the waterfall trails in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 580 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse riverine ecosystems, the well-preserved riparian forests, and the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3855179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toxa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any less crowded waterfall hikes in the region?
A: While the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3855179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Toxa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a popular highlight, exploring some of the longer or more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991760045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, might offer a more secluded experience, especially during off-peak hours or seasons.

Q: What should I bring for a waterfall hike in Sistema Fluvial Ulla-Deza?
A: For waterfall hikes, it's recommended to bring waterproof or water-resistant footwear with good grip, as trails near waterfalls can be damp and slippery. Layers of clothing are advisable, as the mist from waterfalls can make the air cooler. Don't forget water, snacks, and a camera to capture the scenic views.
tips: The exact date of the current bridge is not known, even though historians place its construction around the 14th-15th centuries, replacing or modifying an earlier bridge, possibly Roman, related to the 19th road of the Antonine itinerary, which connected Braga with Lugo and crossed the "Pons Vellegia" (Pontevea) coming from Aquis Celenis (Cuntis).
Paserela Mariola suspended pedestrian bridge, over the Ulla river in Xirimbao is now a famous recreational area. The bridge was built in 1964 by the engineer José Darque for the then dictator Franco

waypoints.name: Xirimbao Suspension Bridge Over the Ulla River
